problem.title: Add and Search Word
problem.description: Design a data structure that supports adding new words and finding if a string matches any previously added string.
assumptions: '.' in search pattern matches any single letter.

example.input: addWord("bad"), addWord("dad"), addWord("mad"), search("pad"), search("bad"), search(".ad"), search("b..")
example.explanation: '.ad' matches any char in position 0, so it matches 'bad','dad','mad'. 'b..' matches 'bad'.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Trie with DFS
solutions.description: Words are stored in a Trie so that prefix lookups are efficient. The wildcard character '.' is handled during search by recursively trying every non-null child at the current Trie level via DFS. This approach is correct because '.' can match any single character, and DFS explores all valid continuations without re-traversing already-matched prefixes.

complexity.time: O(n) for add, O(26^m) for search
